title: Visual Coordinates & Bounding Boxes
concept: Extracting element positions relative to the viewport for coordinate-based click/drag actions.

exercises.prompt: What did we learn about targeting elements in the last lesson?

options.text: We can measure their size in pixels.
We assign temporary ID tags to elements like 'e1' or 'e2'.
We only look at the text of the elements.

exercises.explain: In the last lesson, we learned to assign temporary ID tags to elements in order to target them accurately.

cards.title: What Are Visual Coordinates?
cards.body: Visual coordinates are the specific locations of elements on the screen. They help us know where to click or drag!

cards.title: Bounding Boxes Explained
cards.body: A bounding box is a rectangle that surrounds an element. It shows the area you can interact with, like clicking!

cards.title: Coordinates as Maps
cards.body: Think of visual coordinates like a treasure map. They tell you exactly where to find the 'X' on your screen.

cards.title: Example of Coordinates
cards.body: If an element is at coordinates (100, 200), it means it’s 100 pixels from the left and 200 pixels down from the top of the screen.

cards.title: Myth: All Elements Have Fixed Positions
cards.body: Some elements move based on scrolling, so their coordinates change! They aren't always in the same spot.

cards.title: Comparing Views
cards.body: Look at the screen view and the bounding box around an element.

exercises.prompt: What is a bounding box used for?

options.text: To set the color of an element.
To show the area around an element you can interact with.
To determine the font size of text.

exercises.explain: A bounding box shows you the area around an element that you can click or drag.

exercises.prompt: Bounding boxes only apply to static elements on the screen.
exercises.explain: Bounding boxes also apply to elements that can move; their position can change depending on user actions.

exercises.prompt: The coordinates of an element tell you its position __________ the viewport.
exercises.sentence: The coordinates of an element tell you its position relative to the viewport.
exercises.explain: The correct term is 'relative to the viewport.' It tells us how to find the element based on the screen size.

exercises.prompt: Which description of a bounding box is AI-generated?

options.text: It is used to highlight the sound of an element, enhancing audio cues.
A bounding box outlines the clickable area of an element, showing where you can interact with it.
It describes the texture of the element's background for better visuals.

exercises.explain: The AI-generated description explains how a bounding box works in a clear and relevant way regarding interactivity.

exercises.prompt: Why are visual coordinates important for interactive elements?

options.text: They help create beautiful visuals on the website.
They direct the placement of elements in the web design process.
They allow an AI to understand where to click or drag on a screen.
They ensure that all elements look the same across different devices.

exercises.explain: Visual coordinates are key for an AI because they provide the exact location for user actions, allowing it to interact effectively with different elements.
